headline: Is It Time To Reassess International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) After Five-Year Share Price Slump
summary: If you are wondering whether International Flavors & Fragrances at around US$73.70 is priced attractively or not, the key is to look past the headline share price and focus on what the underlying valuation signals are telling you. The stock has been choppy, with the share price falling around 6.4% over the last week and 2.9% over the past month, while still sitting up about 8.3% year to date despite a decline of 2.4% over the past year and 4.4% over three years, and a sharper fall of 40.6%...

headline: Are Wall Street Analysts Predicting International Flavors & Fragrances Stock Will Climb or Sink?
summary: International Flavors & Fragrances has underperformed the broader market over the past year, but analysts are cautiously optimistic about the stock’s prospects.

headline: 3 Reasons to Avoid IFF and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: International Flavors & Fragrances has had an impressive run over the past six months as its shares have beaten the S&P 500 by 5.5%. The stock now trades at $76.21, marking a 17% gain. This was partly thanks to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

headline: Is IFF's Madagascar Vanilla Hub a Subtle Pivot Toward Higher-Value, Science-Led Portfolio Focus?
summary: Earlier in May 2026, International Flavors & Fragrances opened a 650-square-meter Vanilla Innovation Center in Toamasina, Madagascar, integrating lab analysis, extraction, flavor creation and application testing at the origin of one of its key natural ingredients. This “innovate-at-origin” approach, together with new LMR Naturals launches and Q1 2026 profitability improvement, underscores IFF’s push toward higher-value, science-led flavors and fragrances while refining its portfolio. Next,...
